steps.title: Use the two charge points
steps.content: The answer choices are linear functions of the number of lessons, so use two given data points. From $(8,240)$ to $(18,590)$, the slope is $m=\frac{590-240}{18-8}=35.$ Substituting a point into $y=mx+b$ gives $b=-40$.

steps.title: Interpret the calculation
steps.content: The slope $35$ means each additional lesson adds 35 dollars. The intercept $-40$ is not a literal fee here; it is the intercept of the simplified linear rule for $x\gt 3$. The graph still has to pass through the two real charge points, and $y=35x-40$ does.

steps.title: Write $f(x)$
steps.content: Use the fitted equation as the function: $f(x)=35x-40$. Check the two given points: $f(8)=35(8)-40=240$ and $f(18)=35(18)-40=590$.
